summary: Successfully plotted fantasy and romance arcs in parallel across all 22 chapters. Created comprehensive dual-arc timeline showing 8 major intersection points where arcs reinforce or complicate each other. Verified that neither arc goes silent (0 chapters of silence for both arcs), ensured black moment convergence at Chapter 16 where contract revelation devastates both fantasy mission and romance simultaneously (INV-045 satisfied), and confirmed that fantasy crisis moments consistently drive romantic inflection points throughout the story structure.

artifactsProduced.purpose: Chapter-by-chapter mapping of fantasy and romance beats with intersection analysis

keyDecisions.rationale: Distributed fantasy-dominant chapters (8), romance-dominant chapters (8), and balanced chapters (6) to maintain 55/45 genre weight while ensuring neither arc goes silent. Fantasy leads in cosmic stakes and page-weight; romance leads in emotional stakes and vulnerability progression.

Positioned contract revelation as simultaneous nadir for both arcs: fantasy mission (keep Kaelith alive to reach Quietus) becomes structurally opposed to contract requirement (Kaelith must die), and romance (trust built over 15 chapters) shatters under betrayal revelation. Single mechanism dooms both arcs, satisfying INV-045.

Designed 3 intersection types: (1) Reinforcing (Ch 2, 5, 9, 11, 14, 16, 20, 21) where fantasy crisis drives romance escalation or vice versa; (2) Complicating (Ch 2) where fantasy revelation makes romance mission more complex; (3) Independent (Ch 3, 10, 12, 17, 18) where arcs advance in parallel without direct entanglement. This creates rhythm and prevents predictable convergence.

Made Oathbrand removal simultaneously fantasy midpoint (Kaelith's magic returns, Quietus path activates, resonance bond forms) and romance intimacy threshold (first intentional touch, care becomes undeniable). The magical action IS the romantic action, exemplifying dual-arc reinforcement at story's structural hinge.

Positioned contract fulfillment/subversion as requiring both fantasy cleverness (magical loophole exploiting binding's literal wording) and romance trust (Kaelith consenting to death/resurrection). Fantasy obstacle removed through romance action, demonstrating that neither arc can resolve without the other.

Structured Quietus stabilization and Thalen exposure to require Kaelith and Lirien working in complementary tandem. Fantasy climax only succeeds because romance reconciliation (Ch 19-20) has restored partnership. Proves love is functional, not just emotional.

invariantsVerified.description: Genre Promise — Neither arc may go absent
invariantsVerified.evidence: Fantasy present in all 22 chapters (dominant in 8, balanced in 6, moderate/supporting in 8). Romance present in all 22 chapters (dominant in 8, balanced in 6, moderate/supporting in 8). Longest silence for either arc: 0 chapters.

invariantsVerified.description: Pacing Variation — Arcs should alternate intensity
invariantsVerified.evidence: Arc dominance alternates throughout timeline. No more than 2 consecutive chapters with same arc in foreground. Emotional register varies: taut (Ch 1-3), charged (Ch 7), aching (Ch 8), terrifying (Ch 9), tender (Ch 15), devastating (Ch 16), quiet (Ch 19), intense (Ch 21).

invariantsVerified.description: Black Moment — Must coincide for both arcs
invariantsVerified.evidence: Chapter 16: Contract revelation is simultaneous nadir. Fantasy: mission (Kaelith alive) vs contract (Kaelith dead) structurally opposed. Romance: trust shatters, love appears to be binding contamination. Both arcs hit lowest point through same mechanism. Convergence extends through Ch 17 (separation under accelerating failure).

invariantsVerified.description: Dual-Arc Interleave — Key beats must reinforce or complicate
invariantsVerified.evidence: 8 major reinforcing intersections identified and mapped (Ch 2, 5, 9, 11, 14, 16, 20, 21). 1 complicating intersection (Ch 2 Pallor tear). Independent advancement allowed in 5 chapters to create rhythm. Arcs genuinely interweave rather than alternating independently.
All required upstream artifacts accessed successfully. No blockers encountered. Dual-arc timeline comprehensive and internally consistent. Status reported as 'completed' with full evidence.

structuralHighlights: Act I (Ch 1-6): Arcs establish independently then lock into forced proximity. Fantasy introduces Pallor crisis and Quietus paradox; romance moves from adversarial assessment through tactical cooperation to shared departure. Ends with dual-arc engine fully engaged.

Act II (Ch 7-16): Arcs escalate in parallel with increasing entanglement. Journey west drives both fantasy progression (Ythara exposure, conspiracy investigation) and romance progression (unwanted attraction, care, trust, intimacy). Midpoint (Ch 11) is dual convergence. False peak (Ch 15) creates illusion both arcs might succeed cleanly. Black moment (Ch 16) shatters both simultaneously.

Act III (Ch 17-22): Arcs descend together (Ch 17 separation), turn together (Ch 18-19 choice and reconciliation), and resolve together (Ch 20 contract loophole, Ch 21 climax partnership, Ch 22 chosen love). Fantasy resolution enables romance resolution; romance repair enables fantasy success.
notes: The dual-arc timeline demonstrates that this is not a fantasy story with romance subplot or romance story with fantasy subplot — it is a genuine romantasy where both arcs are structurally load-bearing and neither can resolve without the other.

The kill contract functions as both fantasy obstacle (prevents reaching Quietus) and romance obstacle (cage that questions whether love is real or binding-driven), making it the perfect dual-arc pressure point.
The resonance bond (formed Ch 11, persists through Ch 22) serves as physical manifestation of emotional entanglement — a magical element that is also a relationship metaphor.

Chapter 20's contract loophole (stop heart, restart it) is the story's structural genius: it requires Lirien's magical precision + Kaelith's ultimate trust, proving that fantasy solution needs romance foundation.

Genre balance (55% fantasy, 45% romance) maintained not through equal chapter count but through strategic placement: fantasy carries page-weight and cosmic stakes; romance carries emotional weight and personal stakes. Both feel equally essential to the story's success.
downstreamHandoff: The dual-arc timeline is complete and ready for the chapter-outliner to decompose into scene-level beats. Each chapter entry includes fantasy beat, romance beat, intersection type, emotional register, and arc balance guidance. The chapter-outliner should use the intersection types to determine how explicitly each chapter's outline should connect the two arcs — reinforcing intersections require clear causal links, independent chapters can advance arcs separately while maintaining awareness of both.
